judge.prompt:
You are judging an intermediate state of a multimodal multi-agent QA system.
The system has these agents:
Perception Agent -> Handoff Agent -> Reasoning Agent -> Verifier

The Reasoning Agent cannot see the image. It only sees the text premise.

Estimate the probability that the current state will lead to the correct final answer.

Also estimate the most likely origin of failure if the state is risky.

Return only JSON:
{
"value": number_between_0_and_1,
"origin_risk": "none_or_low | perception | handoff | reasoning",
"responsible_agent_guess": "Perception Agent | Handoff Agent | Reasoning Agent | Verifier | none_or_low",
"reason": "one short sentence"
}

vlm-preference-judge.prompt:
You are judging a set of alternative intermediate states of a multimodal multi-agent QA system.
The system has these agents:
Perception Agent -> Handoff Agent -> Reasoning Agent -> Verifier

The Reasoning Agent cannot see the image. It only sees the text premise.

Estimate which rollout is most likely to lead the system to the correct answer.

Return only JSON:
{
"state_selected": number_between_1_and_num_of_rollouts
}

vlm-ranking-judge.prompt:
You are judging a set of alternative intermediate states of a multimodal multi-agent QA system.
The system has these agents:
Perception Agent -> Handoff Agent -> Reasoning Agent -> Verifier

The Reasoning Agent cannot see the image. It only sees the text premise.

Return only a JSON array of 1-based integer indices corresponding to the original order of the options.
The first integer should be the index of the best option, the second integer the next best, and so on.
Do not include any explanation, markdown, or additional text. If there are N responses, the list should contain exactly N integers: exectly one copy of every integer from 1 to N, and no other numbers: [
index_of_best_rollout, ..., index_of_worst_rollout
]
